reflection.quote: Summer afternoon—summer afternoon; to me those have always been the two most beautiful words in the English language.
reflection.reading:
June 1st. The first real day of summer for a lot of us — the day the calendar finally catches up to what the air has been hinting at. When I was drinking, summer was just another reason. Patios, weddings, long weekends, the sun staying up until 10. Every season had its excuse built in, but summer was the loudest one. I lost a lot of Junes to being hungover before noon and looking forward to night the way someone in a cell looks forward to lights out.

The strange thing nobody told me is that sober summer is louder than drunk summer ever was. The light is brighter. The lake is colder. The bike ride home at dusk actually registers. You remember the conversation on the dock. You remember who was there. You wake up on a Saturday and the whole day belongs to you — not the back half of it after you stitch yourself together, the whole thing.

If you're new to this and dreading what summer is supposed to look like without a drink in your hand, I'd just say: give it one. One full summer of being awake for it. Not perfect, not productive, just present. You don't have to earn the season. You just have to show up for it, the way you couldn't before.
reflection.meditation: Today, let yourself notice one ordinary thing about this season that you would have missed before — the light, the warmth, a conversation that lasts longer than it had to. That's the life. That's the whole thing.

affirmations: I don't have to escape this season — I get to be inside of it.
Today I have the whole day, not just the part I can salvage.
I am building a life I don't need a night off from.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Monday I can actually feel — the start of a week, the start of a month, the start of a summer I'll remember. Grateful for the quiet mornings that used to be impossible, and for the friends who keep showing up whether the calendar says celebrate or not. Grateful that the small things — coffee, sunlight, a clear head — still count, and still add up.
gratitude.closing: One more day. One more season. Awake for it this time.
